plugin_desc: Aruba-specific SNMP collection for switches (ArubaOS-CX). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects ARUBAWIRED private OIDs for device health: per-module CPU and memory utilization, temperature sensors, fan and power-supply status. Aruba reports memory as a utilization percentage directly; fan/psu states are normalized to the unified 1=normal convention. Supported brands/models — Aruba / HPE ArubaOS-CX and ArubaOS-Switch (some formerly HP ProCurve; 2530/2930/3810/5400/6300/8300, etc.).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the Aruba device control plane (ARUBAWIRED per-module CPU utilization), normalized to a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the Aruba device (ARUBAWIRED per-module memory utilization), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the temperature reading of each Aruba hardware sensor (ARUBAWIRED temperature sensor), in degrees Celsius. Abnormally high temperatures may indicate fan failure, blocked airflow, or excessive ambient heat, and can trigger thermal protection or hardware damage if left unaddressed.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each Aruba cooling fan, normalized to the unified convention where 1 means normal and any other value indicates an abnormal/fault state. A non-normal state indicates a cooling fault that can quickly lead to overheating and should be investigated immediately.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each Aruba power supply unit, normalized to the unified convention where 1 means normal and any other value indicates an abnormal/fault state. A non-normal state signals a power redundancy or hardware fault and must be remediated promptly.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
